gazette: Official Journal of the EU-OJ L 123, 8.5.2023
title: EU bans use of lead in PVC
bodyHtml:
On May 4, 2023, the European Commission adopted measures to ban the use and placing on the market of lead in polyvinyl chloride (PVC) products, including imports, in order to protect people and the environment from the toxic effects of lead. Lead compounds are primarily used in PVC in the construction sector, such as window profiles, pipes and tubes, as well as wires and cables. According to the relevant regulation, the lead content in PVC products shall not exceed 0.1%. The restriction will be effective from November 29, 2024.

The restriction, adopted under the European Union's chemical legislation REACH, is expected to prevent up to 8.4 tons of lead emissions per year, benefiting the health of individuals, especially children. Furthermore, it will create a level playing field between PVC products produced in the lead-free EU since 2015 and imported products that may still contain lead. However, products containing recycled rigid PVC may be sold and used for a certain period to promote recycling and achieve an annual saving of 1.5 million tons of CO2 emissions.

The Commission will continue to work with the European Chemicals Agency to explore ways to limit environmental and human exposure to lead in line with the goals of the Chemicals Strategy for Sustainability and the European Green Deal.

For the relevant EU Regulation (EU) 2023/923, click here.
